history.copiedData: A paragraph is a self-contained unit of discourse in writing that deals with a particular point or idea. It typically consists of a group of related sentences—or sometimes a single sentence—and serves as a fundamental building block for organizing extended segments of prose.Key CharacteristicsTo ensure a paragraph is effective and coherent, it usually contains the following elements:Topic Sentence: This sentence introduces the main idea or controlling idea of the paragraph.Supporting Details: These sentences provide evidence, statistics, examples, or explanations to back up the topic sentence.Concluding Sentence: A final sentence that sums up the main point, transitions to the next idea, or provides closure.Structure & Length. Key CharacteristicsTo ensure a paragraph is effective and coherent, it usually contains the following elements:Topic Sentence: This sentence introduces the main idea or controlling idea of the paragraph.Supporting Details: These sentences provide evidence, statistics, examples, or explanations to back up the topic sentence.Concluding Sentence: A final sentence that sums up the main point, transitions to the next idea, or provides closure.Structure & LengthThere is no fixed length for a paragraph, but standard rules apply depending on the format:Academic Writing: Paragraphs usually range from 100 to 200 words, often spanning 5 to 7 sentences.Journalism/Digital: Paragraphs can be much shorter, sometimes consisting of just one sentence for readability.For formatting, paragraphs are traditionally in
SELECT * FROM users WHERE is_admin = true;
